rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2008 LINE 10. CROSS-DEFENDANT ROCK PLICHCIK'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT: The Court is not aware of its application to a motion to strike under CCP § 425.16. Moreover, the decision in Mann v. Quality Old Time Service Inc. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 90, 106 holds that adopting such an approach would be contrary to the statutory scheme that requires the Court to deny the motion if the plaintiff or cross complainant can show "a probability of prevailing on any part of its claim." The Mann Court expressly holds that the anti-SLAPP statute is not to be used to like a motion to strike a portion of the complaint/cross complaint. The cross complaint sets forth paragraphs 1 to 135 that are said to relate to each cause of action. As a consequence of this less than ideal pleading, the Court must examine each cause of action to determine whether its thrust is habitability of the premises, actions of the landlord with respect to repairs or rent or the landlord's actions in withdrawing the property from the market pursuant to the Ellis Act. Cross complainant's causes of action 1, 3, 4 and 5 focus on the issue of habitability. Causes of action 2, 9, 11 and 12 combine habitability with other issues of fact but effectively allege that cross complainant's habitability complaints gave rise to the landlord's subsequent acts. As a consequence, the Court cannot conclude that these causes of action are premised on protected activity. Moreover, the decision in Birkner v. Lam (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 275, 282 holds: "In general, [t]erminating a tenancy or removing property from the rental market are not activities taken in furtherance of the constitutional rights of petition or free speech." =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2008 LINE 10. CROSS-DEFENDANT JACQUELINE PIERI'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT: Pieri's demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Cross complainants are to clarify that Ms. Plichcik is Ms. Pieri and is the owner of the subject property and has a landlord tenant relationship with cross complainant. Cross complainants are required to set forth facts reflecting a duty of care with respect to the negligence claim. Cross complainants are required to plead fraud with particularity, specifically Ms. Pieri's statements and cross complainants' justifiable reliance thereon. Cross complainants are to alleged facts in support of the intentional infliction claim and the trespass claim. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2008 LINE 10. CROSS-DEFENDANT JACQUELINE PIERI'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT: The ruling on Rock's motion is equally applicable to Pieri's motion to strike. MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. SEE TENTATIVE RULING ON ROCK PLICHCIK'S MOTION TO STRIKE. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2008 LINE 10. CROSS-DEFENDANT ROCK PLICHCIK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT: Rock's demurrer is sustained in part with 10 days leave to amend. As to causes of action 1-3 cross complainants are to plead that Ms. Pieri and Ms. Plichcik are the same person. As to the 4th and 5th causes of action, cross complainants must set forth facts as to alter ego, conspiracy or aiding and abetting, if any, beyond the mere allegation of "on-sight (sic) managing property agent." As to the 8th cause of action, cross complainants must allege fraud with particularity as to the installation of the hot water heater. As to the 9th cause of action, cross complainants must allege facts that demonstrate that the cause of action is not barred by the statute of limitations. As to the 9th causes of action, cross complainants must plead with greater specificity than is reflected in paragraph 206. The demurrer to the 10th cause of action is overruled. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: KWAI HO LAM VS. REGINA BIRKNER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2008 LINE 11. DEFENDANTS REGINA BIRKNER, NYRI SCANLON, CHARLES BIRKNER AND WILLIAM BURTON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. BY ACCEPTING RENT FROM APRIL TO AUGUST 2007, THE LANDLORD CREATED A NEW TENANCY. ALTHOUGH THE LANDLORD CONTENDS THIS WAS AN INNOCENT MISTAKE, THE LANDLORD'S EVIDENCE FAILS TO MEET HIS BURDEN OF PERSUASION. THE EVIDENCE OFFERED DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. DEFENDANT'S HEARSAY OBJECTIONS ARE WELL TAKEN IF THE EVIDENCE IS OFFERED FOR THE TRUTH OF THE MATTER STATED. =(302/PJM/VC)
